title: S3 Presigning and CloudFront Signed Access in TypeScript
description: A Node 24 and TypeScript 7 team receives a changed file-delivery case: A product places long-lived S3 presigned URLs in logs, uses one shared CloudFront key, signs with legacy assumptions, and promises immediate revocation after links escape. The existing implementation trusts one compile-time or. Learners cumulatively produce typed signed-delivery contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, presign the exact version, response metadata, method, region, credential scope, and shortest useful expiry while treating the URL as a bearer secret.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose signed URL or signed cookie by object count, path scope, player behavior, client constraints, and leakage surface.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose canned or custom CloudFront policy and constrain expiry, optional start time, resource wildcard, and IP range without accidental overbreadth.
